observations.recommendation: 2. Stop granting bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and exceeding the prescribed rates and refrain from approving disbursements that are tainted with irregularities.

3. Together with all others concerned that henceforth ensure that disbursements against public funds are duly supported with complete and relevant documents before passing in pre-audit and processing the payments.

5. Together with the Municipal Budget Officer, ensure that disbursements for Collective Negotiation Agreement Incentives are properly appropriated, charged and recorded as Other Personnel Benefits under Personnel Services (PS) items and considered in the computation of PS limitations.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) require MAO and GSO to reconcile
the PPE records highlighting the property items involved in the issue with the
submission of justification and/or supporting documents; and (2) adjust immediately
the books of accounts when warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) to submit the necessary documents
substantiating the transactions for dropping of the building accounts by debiting the building
accounts and other payables, otherwise (2) revert the adjustments made, and (3) if indeed
the building accrue to PLGU-Sarangani, course formal transfer of the asset to the latter with
all the necessary papers duly acknowledge by the concerned parties and effect the proper
adjustments.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) adjust the book of accounts by
reverting the adjusting entries made to derecognize the assets; and (2) take up only
proper accounting of derecognition of the assets upon obtaining relevant and sufficient
documentation.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) justify for the insufficiency of cash
and cash equivalents to cover for the outstanding and valid obligations particularly
under General Fund; (2) direct the Local Finance Committee to adopt appropriate
measures to control and monitor not only the appropriations but the cash availability
as well; (3) ensure that 5% Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Fund, 20%
Local Development Fund and other Trust Funds be utilized for the purpose for which
the funds were intended; and (4) strengthen capability to augment income, intensify
collection, impose cost-cutting measures, and prioritize programs, projects and
activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) require Sangguniang Bayan
Secretary’s Office to exhaust all efforts to retrieve copies of the resolutions related to the
opening of the dormant bank accounts to review the purpose for which the said funds where
established, to facilitate the closure of these accounts, and moving forward, (2) the Municipal
Accounting Office to conduct timely review, analysis of the Municipality’s bank accounts to
verify if the purpose of establishing the fund has been completed, discontinued and/or
abandoned and no financial transactions are expected.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit
intact daily or the next banking day to AGDBs all actual collections for the day,
including the issuance of a corresponding memorandum to all field/deputized
collectors to remit to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office all actual collections for the day.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) demand immediately from the
contractor the required documents for prompt liquidation; (2) if project not
completed yet, coordinate with ECCD Council for recommendation of actions for to
be undertaken for the eventual completion of the project and (3) if project is
completed and all related obligation have been paid, return at once the remaining
balance (Php1 million minus Project Contract Price) of the fund transfer.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) justify why Php400,000.00 was
reappropriated to non-20% development PPAs such as PS, MOOE and Subsidy to
operations as apportioned which run counter with the existing DILG Memorandum
Circular; (2) require Local Finance Committee to ensure allocations for 20%
development fund in annual and supplemental budget preparations, and moving
forward, (3) adhere strictly with prevailing laws, rules and regulations concerning the
appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund such as the DILG Memorandum
Circular No. 96-263 dated 09 December 1996.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) undergo criticial processes in
assessing the budgets or allocation to include priority and procurement-and-
implementation ready development projects; (2) require Municipal Development
Council to intensify monitoring and enforcing of implementation of budgeted
development projects to ensure completion within the target dates; (3) provide
justification in its evaluation on development projects abandoned or discontinued and
corresponding appropriations provided to a new development projects; (4) require
Budget Officer to prepare SAAOB showing clearly the development projects
undertaken under current and continuing appropriation as basis for the preparation of
the SCBAA for the period; and moving forward, (5) adhere strictly with provision of law
concerning reversion and reappropriations of funds such as 20% development fund
and the guidelines for the appropriation and utilization including monitoring and
reporting of the 20% development fund.
1. Secure the supporting documents and draw the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to record the adjustments to all the affected accounts.

2. Obtain necessary details for the recorded Local Road Network from Municipal Engineering Office, prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards, and disclose the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
3. Promptly prepare and submit the complete and comprehensive Inventory & Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team for validation.
4. Coordinate and reconcile with each office the proper classification of Accounts recorded in each book as provided in the new Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
5. Reconcile annually the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting records to establish the correct value of PPE at year-end.

6. Make advantage and strictly implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation, and disclosures as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

1. We recommended and Management agreed to exert diligent efforts to send demand letters and cause the collection of these long outstanding Accounts Receivables. Require written communication to document the and responses of the members who received the fund. For those found that collections were remote and could no longer be collectible, evaluate and provide an allowance on impairment and initiate the preparation of request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

1. Together with the MSWDO Head, to enhance the internal control and employ additional mechanisms in the distribution of financial aids to avoid the incurrence of delays in the future releases and payouts.
2. To ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding liquidation adhere to the prescribed guidelines.
1. Direct the preparation of a justification on the deficiencies noted herein and submit a joint explanation on why the subject payment of incentive and bonus should not be disallowed in audit.
4. Ensure that benefits and other compensation of employees and workers are properly deducted and withheld with taxes as prescribed under NIRC and RA 10963.
1. Stop the practice of granting overtime pay to the department heads and assistant department heads in compliance with Item 7.0 of CSC and DBM JC No. 2 s. 2015.

2. For the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that payments for the rendition of overtime services are properly recorded. Authorized overtime pay of employees of the LGU should be recorded under Overtime and Night Pay account while those paid for Job Order Workers should be recorded in the expense account where their relative compensation is charged.

1. Cause the establishment of a written internal guidelines or procedures in the management of cash to address the above deficiencies and gaps, and ensure that they still will be fully disseminated to and implemented by the concerned offices of the Municipal Government.

2. Implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and exert efforts in gathering the necessary supporting documents to enable the recording of the long unadjusted unsettled/unadjusted valid reconciling items as reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
3. Immediately make representations to the respective banks for correction of bank errors noted in the BRS and settlement of bank charges incurred.

1. Prepare a documented representation with the Authorized Government Depository Bank to confirm and verify the existing and non-existing bank accounts under the name of the Municipality and furnish the Accounting Office of their replies to update and make the necessary adjustments in the books accordingly.

1. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and strategize the conduct of the complete physical inventory taking of all the assets and properties in all funds, and submit the RPCPPE to the auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with existing rules and regulations.
2. Direct the Accounting and General Service Office to ensure that reconciliation of all properties be in placed in order to come up with reliable reports.
3. Review the MOA and coordinate with the Accountant of Provincial Government of Sarangani for the proper recognition of the P7.81 million building for its proper recognition.
4. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

1. We recommended to hasten the disposal of all its unserviceable properties in accordance to the rules and regulations of disposal of government properties. Revisit the DBM Manual on Disposal of Government Properties for proper computation of appraised value and COA Circular 89-296 for proper divestment or disposal of properties.
1. The concerned departments are enjoined to be vigilant in the implementation of projects/programs/activities so that constituents can enjoy the benefits due therefrom.

2. For the Municipal Accountant together with the implementing departments to cause the immediate liquidation of the fully implemented programs and projects and return any residual balances to the Source Agencies since unwarranted retention of these funds deprives the donor agencies of resources that could be put into better use in other activities or in other agencies needing assistance.
3. For the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the dormant accounts and make a documented representations with the source agencies for reconciliation and adjustment in the books if warranted.

1. Ensure that insurable properties of the Municipality are insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to protect the same against loss or damage and in case of loss or destruction, the Municipality can at the least recover the cost of these assets.

2. Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the GSIS for property insurance coverage consistent with the provision of COA Circular No. 2018-002 and comply to the submission of updated reports not later than April 30 of each year.
3. Include in the agency annual budget the amount of premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable properties and other assets and ensure its payment to the GSIS.
4. Direct the Municipal Accountant that payments of premium should initially be recognized as prepaid insurance, adjust by the end of the year the expired/used portion on of the insurance premium.

Revisit the provision on supporting documents and on the liquidation of cash advances as cited in COA Circular 2012-001, for the documentary requirements and, Sections 48 (l) and (m) of COA Circular No. 92-382 and adhere thereto.
Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled.

To direct the implementing departments to execute without delay and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU.
Find measures to address the causes of the delayed implementation of projects.
Institute process improvements to achieve compliance with regulations, policies, procedures and other requirements set forth under Rule II, Section 7 of the RA 9184.

Require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances granted to officers and employees at year end. Observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the granting and liquidation of cash advance.

Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 2 PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386.Appropriate funds to defray necessary expenses pertinent with the registration and titling of the real estate occupied by the local government offices.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management, some of which were a reiteration of the prior years’ audit
recommendations, to which they consequently agreed:
For the Municipal Accountant:
1. Secure the supporting documents and draw the necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts to record the adjustments to all the affected accounts.
2. Obtain necessary details for the recorded Local Road Network from Municipal
Engineering Office, prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards, and disclose
the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with
the prescribed format provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
For the General Service Office:
3. Promptly prepare and submit the complete and comprehensive Inventory &
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to the Municipal Accountant and
Audit Team for validation.
For the General Service Office and Municipal Accounting Office
4. Coordinate and reconcile with each office the proper classification of Accounts
recorded in each book as provided in the new Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
For the General Service Office, Municipal Accounting Office, and Inventory
Committee
5. Reconcile annually the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting records
to establish the correct value of PPE at year-end.
6. Make advantage and strictly implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation, and
disclosures as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to exert diligent efforts to send
demand letters and cause the collection of these long outstanding Accounts Receivables.
Require written communication to document the comments and responses of the
members who received the fund. For those found that collections were remote and
could no longer be collectible, evaluate and provide an allowance on impairment and
initiate the preparation of request for authority from the Commission on Audit to
write-off the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December
19, 2016.

With these, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive to which
Management concurred that:
a. Together with the MSWDO Head, to enhance the internal control and employ
additional mechanisms in the distribution of financial aids to avoid the
incurrence of delays in the future releases and payouts; and
b. To ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding liquidation
adhere to the prescribed guidelines.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following courses of action to the
Management to which they have agreed:
For the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan:
a) Direct the preparation of a justification on the deficiencies noted herein and
submit a joint explanation on why the subject payment of incentive and
bonus should not be disallowed in audit;
b) Stop granting bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and
exceeding the prescribed rates and refrain from approving disbursements
that are tainted with irregularities;
For the Municipal Accountant:
a) Together with all others concerned that henceforth ensure that
disbursements against public funds are duly supported with complete and
relevant documents before passing in pre-audit and processing the payments;
b) Ensure that benefits and other compensation of employees and workers are
properly deducted and withheld with taxes as prescribed under NIRC and
RA 10963.
c) Together with the Municipal Budget Officer, ensure that disbursements for
Collective Negotiation Agreement Incentives are properly appropriated,
charged and recorded as Other Personnel Benefits under Personnel Services
(PS) items and considered in the computation of PS limitations.

In view of the discussed observations, we recommended and Management
agreed on the following:
1. Stop the practice of granting overtime pay to the department heads and assistant
department heads in compliance with Item 7.0 of CSC and DBM JC No. 2 s.
2015. Except for the entitlement to overtime pay, elective officials, department
heads and assistant department heads, however, are not precluded from
rendering overtime services whenever the interest of the service requires ;
2. For the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that payments for the rendition
of overtime services are properly recorded. Authorized overtime pay of
employees of the LGU should be recorded under Overtime and Night Pay
account while those paid for Job Order Workers should be recorded in the
expense account where their relative compensation is charged.

For the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Cause the establishment of a written internal guidelines or procedures in the management of cash to address the above deficiencies and gaps, and ensure that they will be fully disseminated to and implemented by the concerned offices of the Municipal Government.
b. Trace the sources of the funds considered dormant and either direct the closure of depository accounts which purpose had already been satisfied to avoid dormancy charges or utilize the fund to its intended purpose.

For the Municipal Accountant:
a. Implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and exert efforts in gathering the necessary supporting documents to enable the recording of the long unsettled/unadjusted valid reconciling items as reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
b. Immediately make representations to the respective banks for correction of bank errors noted in the BRS and settlement of bank charges incurred.

For the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Update cash books and record the valid reconciling entries as evidenced by the journal entry vouchers and bank reconciliation statements received from the Accounting Office as of December 31, 2019 and in the succeeding periods.
b. Cash books must be maintained for all Cash in Bank – Local Currency Savings Account for proper check and balance with accounting office.
c. Monitor regularly the cash balances and transactions to determine proper use of the accounts and prevent charges per BSP regulations for dormant accounts.

a. For the Municipal Assessor to prepare the Assessment Roll and provide the same to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for collection of real property and special education tax;

b. For the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s index and furnish Accounting Office of the duly certified list of taxpayers, their amount due and collectible for the year. Henceforth, such list shall be provided at the beginning of each year as a basis of the Accounting for the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables.

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and strategize the conduct of the complete physical inventory taking of all the assets and properties in all funds, and submit the RPCPPE to the auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with existing rules and regulations;

b. Direct the Accounting and General Service Office to ensure that reconciliation of all properties be in placed in order to come up with reliable reports; and

c. Review the MOA and coordinate with the Accountant of Provincial Government of Sarangani for the proper recognition of the P7.81 million building for its proper recognition.

d. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended to hasten the disposal of all its unserviceable properties in accordance to the rules and regulations of disposal of government properties.

Revisit the DBM Manual on Disposal of Government Properties for proper computation of appraised value and COA Circular 89-296 for proper divestment or disposal of properties.

a. The concerned departments are enjoined to be vigilant in the implementation of the projects/programs/activities so that constituents can enjoy the benefits therefrom.

b. For the Municipal Accountant together with the implementing departments to cause the immediate liquidation of the fully implemented programs and projects and return any residual balances to the Source Agencies since unwarranted retention of these funds deprives the donor agencies of resources that could be put into better use in other activities or in other agencies needing assistance.

c. For the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the dormant accounts and make a documented representations with the source agencies for reconciliation and adjustment in the books if warranted.

d. Henceforth, update the status of project implementation and subsidiary schedules of all accounts to make it more informative, detailed and comprehensive for reference in validation, implementation and disposition of the unutilized amount.

a. Ensure that insurable properties of the Municipality are insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to protect the same against loss or damage and in case of loss or destruction, the Municipality can at least recover the cost of these assets.

b. Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the GSIS for property insurance coverage consistent with the provision of COA Circular No. 2018-002 and comply to the submission of updated reports not later than April 30 of each year; and

c. Include in the agency annual budget the amount of premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable properties and other assets and ensure its payment to the GSIS; and

d. Direct the Municipal Accountant that payments of premium should initially be recognized as prepaid insurance, adjust by the end of the year the expired/used portion of the insurance premium.

For the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct all concerned to see to it that documents needed before preparing vouchers are all in-tact and require the person-in-charge to follow-up lacking documents. Be reminded that payment should be settled within the prescribed period.

a. The Local School Board, being responsible for the formulation, approval and utilization of the SEF, should undertake a comprehensive review of the prior years’ unprogrammed and unutilized SEF and effectively prioritize education programs that complements the education budget of the national government. Idle funds as identified at year end should be appropriated in the next year’s budget based on the appropriation and utilization guidelines on SEF.

b. The Local Treasurer, together with the Local Accountant to undertake comprehensive Cash Analysis and ensure that funds are actually available prior to certifying Statement of Revenue/Income/Savings as fund source of supplemental budgets.
Find measures to address the delayed implementation of projects.
Require the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accounting Offices to follow the guidelines on the appropriate use of the 20% LDF.
Find solution to address the causes of the delayed implementation of projects.
Institute process improvements to achieve compliance with regulations, policies, procedures and other requirements set forth under Rule II, Section 7 of the RA 9184.

Require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances granted to officers and employees at year end. Observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the granting and liquidation of cash advance.

Fast track the implementation of projects that would address the projects that would address the constituents’ resiliency towards disasters envisioned to lessen the impact that may occur in the community.

All programs and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specifics as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken. Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided. Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, project and activity.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and the collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant in order to address the problem in the setting-up of the RPT/SET Receivables.

Effect Adjustments to the affected accounts to conform with the requirements and Facilitate timely submission of JEV's of RPT/SET NGAS Manual.

Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 2 PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386. Appropriate funds to defray necessary expenses pertinent with the registration and titling of the real estate occupied by the local government offices.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and the collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant in order to address the problem in the setting-up of the RPT/SET Receivables. Effect Adjustments to the affected accounts to conform with the requirements and Facilitate timely submission of JEV's of RPT/SET NGAS Manual, Volume 1.

With all of these, we recommended and management agreed on the following courses
of actions:

For the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Cause the establishment of a written internal guidelines or procedures in the
management of cash to address the above deficiencies and gaps, and ensure
that they will be fully disseminated to and implemented by the concerned
offices of the Municipal Government.
b. Trace the sources of the funds considered dormant and either direct the
closure of depository accounts which purpose had already been satisfied to
avoid dormancy charges or utilize the fund to its intended purpose.
For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and exert efforts in
gathering the necessary supporting documents to enable the recording of the
long unsettled/unadjusted valid reconciling items as reflected in the Bank
Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
b. Immediately make representations to the respective banks for correction of
bank errors noted in the BRS and settlement of bank charges incurred.
For the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Update cash books and record the valid reconciling entries as evidenced by
the journal entry vouchers and bank reconciliation statements received from
the Accounting Office as of December 31, 2019 and in the succeeding periods.
b. Cash books must be maintained for all Cash in Bank – Local Currency
Savings Account for proper check and balance with accounting office.
c. Monitor regularly the cash balances and transactions to determine proper
use of the accounts and prevent charges per BSP regulations for dormant
accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and management agreed on the following:
a. For the Municipal Assessor to prepare the Assessment Roll and provide the
same to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for collection of real property and
special education tax;
b. For the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account

duly certified list of taxpayers, their amount due and collectible for the year.
Henceforth, such list shall be provided at the beginning of each year as a
basis of the Accounting for the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables.

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and strategize the conduct of the
complete physical inventory taking of all the assets and properties in all
funds, and submit the RPCPPE to the auditor not later than January 31 of

b. Direct the Accounting and General Service Office to ensure that
reconciliation of all properties be in placed in order to come up with reliable
reports; and
c. Review the MOA and coordinate with the Accountant of Provincial
Government of Sarangani for the proper recognition of the P7.81 million
building for its proper recognition.

In view of the foregoing audit observations, the Audit Team recommended and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the complete bank
reconciliation statements for all depository accounts. Likewise, all should be
submitted as at year-end.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to book the valid reconciling items as reflected in
the Bank Reconciliation Statements. Make adjustment on the stale checks to reflect
the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a. Require the GSO to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Properties (IIRUP) for the unserviceable properties and dispose in accordance with
the guidelines. Provide the MAO with the duly acted IIRUP for appropriate
adjustments in the books.
b. Require the MAO to record all on-going projects to the Construction in Progress
account.

We recommended and management agreed to return the unused and/or excess
funds of the completed projects to the Source Agency for proper valuation/ reconciliation of
accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to find measures to address the causes
of the delayed implementation of projects.

We recommend to management the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to make adjusting entries to reclassify into
the proper accounts which pertain to the on-going infrastructure projects.
b. Require the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accounting Offices to follow the
guidelines on the appropriate use of the 20% LDF.

We recommended and management agreed to find solution to address the causes of
the delayed implementation of projects.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MAO to comply strictly on the
submission of financial transactions/documents within the prescribed period in accordance
with existing regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to institute process
improvements to achieve compliance with regulations, policies, procedures and other
requirements set forth under Rule II, Section 7 of the RA 9184.

observations.reason: On the process of reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to management the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant should pursue his commitment to prepare and continuously submit on a monthly basis the BRS both for current and savings accounts.
b. Prepare adjusting entries on the staled checks and reconciling items in BRS to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommend to management to strictly require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct an analysis and confirmation of dormant accounts and process the termination of the dormant accounts in consistent with COA-DOF-DBM Joint Circular No. 4-2012 and other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.reason: one bank account remained dormant and not terminated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management to require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances granted to officers and employees at year end. Observe strictly the rules and regulations regarding the granting and liquidation of cash advance.
observations.reason: Partial liquidation were made as at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the LCE to instruct the DRRM Officer to fast track the implementation of projects that would address the constituents’ resiliency towards disasters that may occur in the community.

We recommend management to maximize the implementation of the projects, programs and activities under the 20% Development Fund, thru:
a. Finding remedies to cope up with the problem on technical personnel, such as outsourcing the preparation of technical documents;
b. Revert and reprogram programs, projects and activities which are not feasible into more significant projects with consultation with Municipal Development Council; and
c. Review the Program of Work which prices were affected by the recent TRAIN Law and start the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: AAR on the preparation of technical documents of the management. 2015-2017
observations.reason: Some engineers left/resigned, that caused of lack of manpower and backlogs

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management the following:
a. Review the long-outstanding balances of Due to Other NGAs and reconcile and/or settle with the Source Agencies;
b. Henceforth, implement on time the programs, projects and activities of the fund transfers so that the benefits derived therefrom may be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries; and
c. Request authority from the Source Agencies to reprogram the projects for the projects and programs that could no longer be pursued.
observations.reason: not observed resulted to uninterested bidders due to low price; Available suppliers in the market were not PHILGEPS accredited.

observations.recommendation: Reprogram those inappropriate projects to conform to the prescribed criteria.

We recommend management the following:
a. Return to the Source Agency the amount received for which the intended purpose could no longer be pursued and the residual balances of completed projects.
b. Review and analysis the long-outstanding balances of Due to Other NGAs and reconcile with the Source Agencies records and make necessary adjustments if warranted.
c. Monitor and implement timely the programs, projects and activities so that constituents can enjoy the benefits due to them.
observations.reason: Excess fund from completed projects were still exist. Letters from the implementing offices were not made on time, as this was one of the requirements of the MMO before returning the fund to the SA.

a. Review the long-outstanding balances of Due to Other NGAs and reconcile
and/or settle with the Source Agencies;

We recommend the following:
a. Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to locate documents pertinent to the LGU properties preparatory to the conduct of the actual physical inventory; conduct a complete physical count / with existing regulations and in order to establish the actual existence; c. Submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the latter's ledger cards; d. Require the Accounting and GSO to maintain ledger and property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records; and e. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Conducted preparatory to the conduct of the actual physical count, but only those properties under General Fund. Started reconciliation but not yet finished.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:
a. Hire qualified employee who will be responsible in the monitoring of inventory records of the MHO; b. Implement a customized electronic inventory system. It should be programmed in a way that will cater both the needs of the MHO and MAO (e.g. inventories can be sorted based on expiration date, PO, fund source, etc).

We recommend to stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts and to strictly comply with Administrative Order No. 248 and existing regulations of the revised IRR of RA 9184 in connection with the LGU’s procurement activities.

We recommend the following:
a. Refrain from including projects in the 20% LDF which do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and b. Reprogram those inappropriate projects to conform to the prescribed criteria.

We recommend the following:
a. Stop classifying the budget for payment to services of job order personnel and teachers under PS account Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual and classify them instead under Other Professional Services of MOOE in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; b. Use an appropriate CSC appointment form rather than a contract or MOA for Job Orders if management intends to hire contractual personnel; and c. Request LSB to review the succeeding year SEF budget; and if same error persists, a budget reclassification should be made thru an LSB resolution.

We recommend the following:
a. Follow the required work flow on processing requirements to ensure that documents were properly filled up as to reference number and signed by appropriate signatories ; and b. Adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 relative to the documentary requirements for each type of disbursement.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a. Hire qualified employee to address manpower deficiency in the engineering department particularly in the preparation of POW and project supervision; b. Comply promptly to all the requirements by the NGAs providing counterpart funding for projects to be implemented in the Municipality; and c. Maximize the implementation of the various projects, programs and activities under the 20% Development Fund that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a. Utilize optimally available resources to achieve maximum disaster preparedness. Prepare LDRRMFIP that includes current and previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF to maximize the programs, projects and activities relating to mitigation, prevention and preparedness; and b. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to abide with the guidelines provided by COA Circular 2012-002 especially on the inclusion of unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF for Capital Outlay in the continuing appropriation of General Fund books.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a. Return to the Source Agency the amount received for which the intended purpose could no longer be pursued and the residual balances of completed projects. b. Review and analyse the long-outstanding balances of Due to Other NGAs and reconcile with the Source Agencies records and make necessary adjustments if warranted. c. Monitor and implement timely the programs, projects and activities so that constituents can enjoy the benefits due to them.

We recommend to address immediately the lengthy period of action on procurement and conduct time and motion study in the process from requisition up to delivery to identify problem areas relative to the delay.

Determine priority programs affecting the schools in the locality in accordance with pertinent provisions of RA 7160. Available resources should be used to implement identified priority projects that could alleviate the deficiencies in public school facilities of the three (3) Districts of Alabel.

All the planned programs, projects and activities incorporated in the LGU’s regular budget should have been included in the Annual Investment Program. Revisit the guidelines set on the harmonization of local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting, and expenditure management in the DBM-NEDA-DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007.

All programs and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specifics as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken. Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided. Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, project and activity.
We recommend to stop implementing projects chargeable to 20% LDF which do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

a. Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and b. Exert best effort to comply with the requirement of the funding agencies so that funds will be downloaded to the LGU.

Prioritize programs, projects and activities in the ensuing year specifically those mentioned in Section 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160 to address the problems of the students and teachers particularly on school facilities.

Remit to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized balances of PDAF amounting to P800,668.43 for projects that have been fully completed, purposes have lapsed, terms have expired, proponents no longer incumbent and for unimplemented projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a. close all completed projects to the appropriate asset accounts; and b. reclassify the projects totaling P7.793 million that were erroneously recorded under the Public Infrastructure-Artesian Wells, Reservoirs, Pumping Station and Conduits account (217);

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to determine if the amounts totaling P5.66M which correspond to the unobligated balance of the non-implemented and unfinished PDAF projects are among the funds enjoined in the Supreme Court decision, and ensure faithful compliance with said ruling on the immediate reversion of subject PDAF allocations to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund if such is the case.

Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 2 PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386. Appropriate funds to defray necessary expenses pertinent with the registration and titling of the real estate occupied by the local government offices.
observations.reason: however there was suspension of issuance of title
